channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: President Samia to inaugurate construction of 15 new oil storage tanks at Dar es Salaam Port
item.description: DAR ES SALAAM: The President of Tanzania, Samia Suluhu Hassan, is set to officially attend the foundation-laying ceremony for 15 new oil storage tanks at Dar es Salaam Port tomorrow, 3rd March 2026. A...

item.title: US Embassy and Consulates Suspend Services in Pakistan Amid Protests
item.description: The US Mission to Pakistan has cancelled all visa and American Citizen Services (ACS) appointments for March 2 at its Embassy in Islamabad and Consulates in Karachi and Lahore due to security concerns...

item.title: South African Health Department Officials Face Charges of Fraud and Theft
item.description: The Hawks, South Africa’s Directorate for Priority Crime Investigation, has executed arrest warrants against the Health Department’s director-general, Sandile Buthelezi, along with two senior official...

item.title: Australia considers criminalising pro-Palestine phrases without clear context
item.description: Queensland and New South Wales are exploring legislation that could criminalise certain pro-Palestine phrases, such as 'globalise the intifada'. The proposed measures have sparked debate over free spe...

item.title: Pakistan's JF-17 Thunder Gains International Interest, MoU Signed at Dubai Airshow 2025
item.description: Pakistan's JF-17 Thunder has drawn notable attention at the Dubai Airshow 2025, marking a significant achievement for the country's aviation sector. During the event, a memorandum of understanding (Mo...

item.title: President Samia names new ambassadors and reshuffles diplomatic postings
item.description: President Samia Suluhu Hassan has carried out a significant reshuffle of Tanzania’s diplomatic corps. Lieutenant General Mathew Mkingule has been appointed as the new ambassador to Cuba, replacing Hum...

item.title: Senate Confirms New Schedule for BARMM Elections in September 2026
item.description: The Philippine Senate has approved a bill to reset the first regular elections in the Bangsamoro Autonomous Region in Muslim Mindanao (BARMM) to the second Monday of September 2026, with subsequent el...

item.title: Russia and Tanzania Deepen Cooperation in Defence and Education
item.description: Dar es Salaam – Tanzania and Russia dey continue to build strong partnership wey don last for more than 60 years, focusing on defence, education, trade, and political cooperation. The Russian Ambassad...

item.title: How the UK’s trade deficit affects household costs and exposure to global shocks
item.description: The UK’s trade deficit in goods reached a record £248.3 billion in 2025, meaning the country spent far more on imports than it earned from exports. While the UK maintains a strong surplus in services ...

item.title: Green Energy Jobs Are Growing in South Africa, But Inequalities Persist
item.description: South Africa is gradually moving from its heavy reliance on coal towards greener energy, which is helping create new jobs. The country’s economy historically depends on coal, which generates about 70%...

item.title: Nigerian Tugboat Sinks Off South African Coast, One Dead, Five Missing
item.description: A Nigerian tugboat named LEO sank off the South African coastline late on Saturday night, resulting in the death of one crew member, while five remain missing and twelve were rescued. The vessel, whic...

item.title: Filipinos in UAE Face Challenges Amid US-Israel Iran Strikes
item.description: Filipinos living in the United Arab Emirates are experiencing the effects of renewed Middle East tensions after the US and Israel launched airstrikes on Iran, and Iran retaliated with missiles and dro...

item.title: Ontario mall collapse lawsuit in Elliot Lake set to settle for $10 million
item.description: A proposed $10 million settlement has been reached in the lawsuit stemming from the 2012 collapse of the Algo Centre Mall in Elliot Lake, Ontario. The tragic incident, which claimed two lives and inju...

item.title: EFF and SACP collaborate to plan Leftist Conference in South Africa
item.description: The Economic Freedom Fighters (EFF) and the South African Communist Party (SACP) have recently held a closed-door meeting to explore the future of leftist politics in South Africa. The meeting, attend...

item.title: US-Iran Strikes Highlight Lack of Clear Political Strategy
item.description: The recent U.S.-Israeli military strikes on Iran, beginning February 28, 2026, have caused significant destruction, including the death of Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, and extensive bombing ...

item.title: Home Ministry sets up two additional empowered committees in West Bengal to expedite CAA citizenship applications
item.description: The Union home ministry has constituted two additional empowered committees in West Bengal to accelerate the processing of citizenship applications filed under the Citizenship (Amendment) Act, 2019 (C...

item.title: Mewar Royal Family Engaged in Legal Battle Over Ancestral Estate
item.description: In Udaipur, the Mewar royal family is once again in the limelight due to a dispute over ancestral properties, a year after the demise of King Arvind Singh, the last recognised custodian of the estate....

item.title: WNBA proposes higher salaries and faster maximum contracts in new collective bargaining offer
item.description: The WNBA has presented a new collective bargaining agreement (CBA) proposal to its players’ union that aims to increase player salaries and allow rising stars to reach maximum contract eligibility soo...

item.title: India lacks national registry to track six lakh babies born with congenital anomalies annually
item.description: In India, approximately six lakh babies are born every year with congenital anomalies, yet there is no comprehensive national registry to monitor these cases. Experts note that as infectious disease-r...

item.title: Akbayan lauds Tarriela’s promotion for defending West Philippine Sea sovereignty
item.description: Akbayan Party has congratulated Philippine Coast Guard spokesperson Jay Tarriela on his promotion to rear admiral, calling it a symbolic stand against China and the Chinese Embassy in Manila, which ha...
